description: Security hooks: secrets-sentinel prevents accidental writes to sensitive files (.env, keys, credentials); danger-guard confirms destructive commands (rm -rf, force push, DROP TABLE)

hooks.prompt: Check the file_path in $TOOL_INPUT against sensitive file patterns. DENY if the path matches any of these: .env, .env.*, .env.local, .env.production, credentials.json, secrets.json, service-account*.json, *.pem, *.key, *.p12, *.pfx, id_rsa, id_ed25519. ALLOW if the path ends with .env.example, .env.sample, or .env.template (these are safe documentation files). If the file matches a sensitive pattern, respond with 'deny' and explain which sensitive pattern was matched. Otherwise respond with 'allow'.

Check the command in $TOOL_INPUT for writes to sensitive files. DENY if the command uses >, >>, tee, cp, or mv to WRITE to files matching: .env, .env.*, .env.local, .env.production, credentials.json, secrets.json, service-account*.json, *.pem, *.key, *.p12, *.pfx, id_rsa, id_ed25519. ALLOW read-only commands (cat, grep, head, tail, less, diff, file, stat, ls) that reference these files — reading is safe. ALLOW writes to .env.example, .env.sample, .env.template. If the command writes to a sensitive file, respond with 'deny' and explain the risk. Otherwise respond with 'allow'.

Check the command in $TOOL_INPUT for destructive operations. Flag these patterns: rm -rf (except when targeting only node_modules, dist, build, __pycache__, .cache, or coverage), git reset --hard, git push --force or git push -f (especially dangerous if targeting main or master), git branch -D, DROP TABLE, DROP DATABASE, docker system prune, chmod 777. If the command matches a destructive pattern, respond with 'ask' and describe the specific risk (e.g. 'This will permanently delete files' or 'Force pushing to main will rewrite shared history'). For git push --force to main/master, emphasize this is extremely dangerous and can destroy team history. If the command is safe or matches an allowed rm -rf target, respond with 'allow'.
